body.title: Auditor rack device: promote audit_runner.py to first-class rack service
body.result: AuditorDevice rack device: 6 MCP tools (run_check/run_all/check_add/check_list/allowlist_add/finding_history), adc.audit_findings history, 24 tests green
body.description:
audit_runner.py in lab/claudecode/ runs registered checks (grep, sql, shell, python kinds) against the codebase and DB. Promote this to a rack device so all audit skills call it via MCP instead of inline bash. Skills become thin wrappers; the audit logic, check registry, history, and allowlist live in the device.

MCP tools exposed: run_check(name) → finding[]; run_all(severity_min='med') → finding[]; check_add(name, kind, pattern, severity, description); check_list() → check[]; allowlist_add(pattern, reason); finding_history(days=7) → finding[].

Migration: audit_checks.json and audit_runner.py become the device's seed data + implementation. Existing /day-close-audit and /audit-expert skills updated to call mcp__auditor__* instead of bash.

**Affected files:** devices/auditor/ (new), lab/claudecode/audit_runner.py (migrated), lab/claudecode/audit_checks.json (migrated), skills/day-close-audit/SKILL.md, skills/audit-expert/SKILL.md
**Design rules:** BaseDevice/BaseShim; no-sqlite; audit history in Postgres (clan schema or instance schema TBD)
**Scope boundary:** IN — promote existing audit_runner to device; migrate audit skills to MCP calls; OUT — new check types beyond current grep/sql/shell/python kinds
**Completion criteria:** mcp__auditor__run_check(name='no-sqlite-imports') returns same result as current audit_runner; /day-close-audit skill uses MCP calls, not bash; audit history queryable via MCP
